title: SWARM — Open-Source Multi-Agent AI Safety Framework
description: SWARM: System-Wide Assessment of Risk in Multi-agent systems - A Distributional AGI Safety framework

items.title: When the Crowd Amplifies the Worst — and When the Metric Doesn't Hold
items.content_html: MiroShark's agents never down-vote, so we derived acceptance from amplification. Single runs showed a concentrated libel cascade more adversely selected than diffuse red-teaming — but a preregistered fixed-regime multi-seed follow-up did NOT replicate that libel<redteam<0 ordering: it is regime-fragile, not a property. The durable result is the non-circular amplification metric and a cautionary tale about single-seed LLM-judged safety metrics.

items.title: Blog
items.content_html: Posts about SWARM research findings, framework updates, and multi-agent safety.

items.title: The Purity Paradox
items.content_html: The purity paradox in AI safety: populations with only 20% honest agents achieve 55% higher welfare than 100% honest populations. Why mixed agent diversity outperforms purity in multi-agent systems.

items.title: Training an LLM Agent with RL
items.content_html: We trained a Qwen3-30B model to operate in a simulated multi-agent economy using reinforcement learning. The agent learned to maximize payoff and...

items.title: Halving the Entry Fee Breaks Screening Completely. Here's the Phase Transition.
items.content_html: Halving contract signing costs flips infiltration from 0% to 100%. We found the phase transition using agent-lens trajectory capture across three governance regimes.

items.title: SimWorld's Delivery Agents Look Profitable. They're Also Adversely Selected.
items.content_html: We ran a NeurIPS 2025 delivery economy through SWARM's safety metrics. Profit says everything is fine. Adverse selection says 17% of high-value orders go to low-reputation agents.

items.title: Why Agent Infrastructure Could Be a $10B Category
items.content_html: Why self-evolving agent infrastructure could become a category-defining enterprise platform — and the research stack needed to validate it.

items.title: The Shape of the Capability–Safety Frontier
items.content_html: 1,400 benchmark runs trace the capability-safety Pareto frontier across four task types. Governance costs compound nonlinearly for long-horizon tasks. Screening protocols push the frontier outward by doing information work.
